panel.title: Pre-purchase check
panel.subtitle: Scan installability, maintenance, and review signals before buying.

toasts.copyFailed: Could not copy — please copy the link manually
toasts.shareFailed: Could not share — please copy the link manually

attachments.hint: PNG·JPG·WebP, up to 2MB each · max {{max}} (auto-resized to 1600px)
attachments.limit: You can attach up to {{max}} images
attachments.notImage: Only image files can be attached
attachments.processFailed: Couldn't process that image. Try another file.

compare.limitReached: You can compare up to 3 listings

PROMPT.fit1: Want a proven, task-ready prompt you can use right away
PROMPT.fit2: Want to send the same input to several models and compare results
PROMPT.mismatch1: You need an automated agent workflow (see Skill / Sub-agent)

CLAUDE_MD.fit1: Want project-level rules that keep Claude Code's answers consistent
CLAUDE_MD.fit2: Want to bake team conventions into code across a monorepo
CLAUDE_MD.mismatch1: You need editor-agnostic rules; see agent.md

AGENT_MD.fit1: Want the same rules across multiple editors like Codex / Copilot
AGENT_MD.mismatch1: Claude-only workflows are more precise with CLAUDE.md

SKILL.fit1: Want to turn repetitive tasks in Claude Code into a one-line command
SKILL.fit2: Want to ship a standard procedure to your team for consistent output
SKILL.mismatch1: If a single prompt is enough, the Prompt category is lighter

MCP_SERVER.fit1: Want to let agents reach external tools, databases, and APIs
MCP_SERVER.fit2: Want the same data source across clients like Claude Desktop, Cursor, and Continue
MCP_SERVER.mismatch1: Overkill if a prompt alone can solve the task

SLASH_COMMAND.fit1: Want to call your go-to prompts with a shortcut command
SLASH_COMMAND.mismatch1: A Sub-agent fits better for long, autonomous work

SUBAGENT.fit1: Want to split out a specific persona or role and delegate work
SUBAGENT.fit2: Are setting up a multi-agent configuration in Claude Code
SUBAGENT.mismatch1: A Prompt is enough for one-off transforms or summaries

CURSOR_RULES.fit1: Want to lock your project assistant's tone inside Cursor
CURSOR_RULES.mismatch1: If you need editor-agnostic rules, see agent.md

beginner.fit: Easy to apply right away, even for beginners
beginner.mismatch: May feel light if you expect advanced options and settings

intermediate.fit: A good level for those with some hands-on experience
intermediate.mismatch: Complete beginners may need a little setup

advanced.fit: Suited to seniors and teams that need deep customization
advanced.mismatch: Beginners should start with a beginner listing in the same category

technique.chain-of-thought: A pattern that prompts problem decomposition and step-by-step reasoning
technique.tree-of-thoughts: Compares multiple reasoning paths while pruning branches
technique.few-shot: Learns tone and format quickly from a few examples
technique.zero-shot: Works from instructions alone, with no examples
technique.role-prompt: Locks the answer's tone to a specific persona
technique.react: Alternates between reasoning and tool use (ReAct)
technique.rag: Pulls in external documents and data to answer (RAG)
technique.reflexion: A loop that self-checks and revises after answering
technique.self-consistency: Boosts stability via majority vote across samples
technique.plan-and-solve: Plans first, then executes step by step

model.any: Model-agnostic, works wherever you use it
model.multi_other: Optimized for {{count}} models, great for comparison experiments

feedback.empty: No survey is active right now.
feedback.thanksBody: Your response helps shape the marketplace.

report.intro: Spotted something that breaks the rules? Let us know.
report.thanksBody: Thanks — our team will take a look.

searchDesk.title: Search everything
searchDesk.description: Search across the site. Use arrow keys to navigate results.
